title: Manage Authenticator Lifecycle
description: Employ authenticator management by establishing initial authenticator content, ensuring administrative procedures are in place for lost/compromised or damaged authenticators, and changing authenticators at defined periods.

checks.name: Password Expiration Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify maximum password age is set to a defined period (typically 60-90 days) in the local or domain password policy

checks.name: Passwords Must Be Changed at First Logon
checks.purpose: Confirm newly created accounts are set to require a password change at next logon to prevent use of initial/temporary credentials

checks.name: Account Lockout Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify account lockout threshold, duration, and observation window are configured to limit credential brute-force attempts

checks.name: Password Expiration Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify maximum password age is set to a defined period in the domain or local password policy

checks.name: Passwords Must Be Changed at First Logon
checks.purpose: Confirm newly created accounts are flagged to require a password change at next logon

checks.name: Account Lockout Policy is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify account lockout threshold, duration, and observation window are configured via domain password policy or Fine-Grained Password Policy

checks.name: Service Account Passwords are Managed and Rotated
checks.purpose: Confirm service account passwords are rotated on a defined schedule or use gMSA for automatic rotation

checks.name: Password Maximum Age is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify PASS_MAX_DAYS in /etc/login.defs is set to a defined period (typically 60-90 days)

checks.name: Password Minimum Age is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm PASS_MIN_DAYS in /etc/login.defs is set to at least 1 day to prevent immediate password recycling

checks.name: Account Lockout is Configured via PAM
checks.purpose: Verify pam_tally2 or pam_faillock is configured in /etc/pam.d/ to lock accounts after a defined number of failed attempts

checks.name: Password Warning Period is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm PASS_WARN_AGE in /etc/login.defs is set to warn users before their password expires

checks.name: Password Maximum Age is Configured
checks.purpose: Verify PASS_MAX_DAYS in /etc/login.defs is set to a defined period on Debian

checks.name: Password Minimum Age is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm PASS_MIN_DAYS in /etc/login.defs is set to at least 1 day on Debian

checks.name: Account Lockout is Configured via PAM faillock
checks.purpose: Verify pam_faillock is configured in /etc/pam.d/common-auth to lock accounts after failed attempts on Debian

checks.name: Password Warning Period is Configured
checks.purpose: Confirm PASS_WARN_AGE in /etc/login.defs is set to warn users before password expiry on Debian

remediation: Set maximum password age to 60-90 days via GPO or domain password policy. Enable forced password change at first logon for new accounts. Configure account lockout after 5 failed attempts. On Linux, set PASS_MAX_DAYS, PASS_MIN_DAYS, and PASS_WARN_AGE in /etc/login.defs and configure pam_faillock in /etc/pam.d/.
